SlideVisualDesign.description: Layout and visual design produced by Step 5. The LLM designs every slide's layoutSpec from first principles based on its specific content, narrative role, and audience — there is no pattern catalog to select from. Consumed by Step 6 (content fitting) and Step 7 (PPTX generation).

structuralFamily.description: Visual family declaration for structural slides (cover, closing, dividers). The LLM designs one shared visual treatment per deck and every cover/closing/divider must match it, so structural slides share visual DNA.

slides.description: Ordered list of slide tasks. One entry per slide in the deck, matching s03-presentation-blueprint.json order.

imageryDemandRefinedAt.description: Optional Step 5b override of the s02.imageryDemand value. Written when the resolved contentDomain/register strongly contradicts the brief's value (typically when the brief defaulted to medium with low confidence). The Step 5 validator reads newValue before falling back to s02.imageryDemand.

imageryFloorWaiver.description: Optional deck-level waiver of the Imagery Floor (visual-tone-mapping.md § Imagery Floor). When present with reason >= 20 chars, the validator skips the floor check.

StructuralFamily.description: Visual family declaration for structural slides. The LLM designs this family once per deck (in Step 5a) so cover, closing, and dividers share visual DNA. All fields below are LLM-authored descriptive specifications — none is a lookup into a pattern catalog.

sharedMotif.description: Short descriptive name for the recurring decorative motif the LLM designed for this deck's structural slides (e.g. 'thin-accent-bar-under-title', 'left-side-stripe', 'corner-block-tr'). Inline shape definitions for the motif live in each structural slide's layoutSpec.zones.

coverTreatment.description: Brief description of cover visual treatment (e.g. 'gradient bg + display title + wide accent bar').

closingTreatment.description: Brief description of closing visual treatment (e.g. 'gradient bg + display title + same accent bar, reduced decoration').

dividerPattern.description: Descriptive name for the LLM-designed divider layout used across this deck (e.g. 'left-stripe-section-title'). Every divider slide's layoutPattern must match this name; their layoutSpec.zones must encode the same composition.

motifRationale.description: Why this motif was chosen — must reference narrative architecture signals (storytelling pattern, transition strategy, audience), not aesthetic preferences.

coverBackgroundType.description: Declared background type for the cover slide. The cover's layoutSpec.background.type must match this declaration. Driven by s02.imageryDemand (with optional Step 5b override via imageryDemandRefinedAt) and the Imagery Floor (visual-tone-mapping.md § Dimension 6 Imagery Floor): demand=high → image-generated; demand=medium → image-generated or gradient; demand=low/none → any.

closingBackgroundType.description: Declared background type for the closing slide. The closing's layoutSpec.background.type must match this declaration. Same demand-driven defaults as coverBackgroundType.

dividerBackgroundType.description: Declared background type for divider slides. Every divider slide's layoutSpec.background must match. 'motif-shape' means the divider relies on strong shape decoration over a solid/tinted base (no image generation). Imagery Floor: demand=high → image-generated; demand=medium → image-generated or motif-shape; demand=low → motif-shape acceptable.

recapBackgroundType.description: Declared background type for recap slides. Every recap slide's layoutSpec.background must match. Recap slides serve a rhythmic-pause role like dividers; the Imagery Floor treats them on the divider row. Omit when the deck has no recap slide. Under the structural-slide imagery uplift at imageryDemand=medium, every recap MUST be image-generated.

agendaDecorationFromMotif.description: When true, every agenda slide must include at least one shape zone whose role derives from sharedMotif (e.g. accent-bar, side-stripe, corner-block) so the agenda inherits the deck's visual DNA instead of presenting as bare text on a solid background. Recommended (and validator-enforced) for technical/analytical decks under the structural-slide imagery uplift.

bookendSignature.description: Optional but recommended. Declares an explicit visual 'rhyme' between the cover and the closing slide so the two highest-impact frames in the deck answer each other. Validator emits a warning when absent and enforces field shape + a structural cover↔closing zone-role overlap when present.

BookendSignature.description: Declares the visual rhyme between the cover and the closing slide (workflow-step5.md § Bookend signature).

rhyme.description: Short descriptive name for the visual rhyme (e.g. 'mirrored-light-source', 'inverted-stripe', 'warm-to-cool-flip').

coverExpression.description: One sentence describing how the rhyme manifests on the cover slide.

closingExpression.description: One sentence describing how the rhyme manifests on the closing slide — the visual answer to the cover.

designIntent.description: Why this rhyme reinforces the deck's coreArgument / closingAnchor. Aesthetic taste alone is not a sufficient reason.

SlideTask.description: A single slide's LLM-designed layout and visual specification.

title.description: Working title carried forward from blueprint.

slideType.description: Structural type carried forward from blueprint.

narrativeRole.description: Narrative function carried forward from blueprint. Null for divider and similar non-narrative slides.

narrativeIntent.description: Narrative-level delivery mode carried forward from blueprint. Informs the LLM's design but does not select a template.

layoutPattern.description: Descriptive name the LLM assigns after designing the layout (e.g. 'asymmetric-stat-hero-band', 'five-step-flow-icons'). Every layout is designed from content first, then named for traceability. The name serves cross-slide consistency checks (e.g. divider sharing) and human readability. Slides with similar content structures may intentionally share a name when justified in designRationale.

emphasis.description: Visual emphasis level derived from narrativeRole + slideType. high=climax/cover/closing, standard=evidence/analysis/setup, low=divider.

oneOf.description: Combined rationale (allowed only for cover, divider, and closing slides). Body slides MUST use the dual-track object form.

narrative.description: What about the content/narrative drove this layout. References act, narrativeRole, headlineMessage, accentIntensity, informationDensity.

narrativeLink.description: How this slide's design serves the slide's narrative purpose. MUST contain either the slide's narrativeRole token (e.g. 'climax', 'pivot', 'evidence') or a content word (>=4 chars) from the blueprint headlineMessage. Generic phrasing like 'supports the message' is rejected by the validator.

focalPoint.description: The single visual element the eye lands on first. Name a concrete element ('central architecture diagram', 'left-side stat block', 'full-width quote'). Generic adjectives like 'clean', 'professional', 'balanced', 'modern' are rejected.

eyePath.description: Ordered list of 2–4 visual stops describing how the eye moves across the slide (e.g. ['title', 'central diagram', 'right-side implications']).

aestheticMove.description: The specific deliberate visual gesture this slide makes. Replaces the legacy free-form 'aesthetic' string with a structured triple.

move.description: The concrete visual gesture (e.g. '60/40 asymmetric split with display-weight stat dominating left', 'full-height dark code frame against light explanation panel', 'display title in accent color over near-empty page'). Avoid functional restatements like 'two-column for clarity' — the validator only enforces non-empty; quality of this string is a Step 8b QA concern.

axes.description: Which of the design-philosophy reasoning axes the move engages. At least 1, at most 3.

reason.description: Why this specific move serves the slide's headlineMessage and narrativeRole. Generic reasons like 'visual variety' or 'looks better' are rejected.

quotaWaiver.description: Optional. Records which Universal Design Floor quota was waived for this slide and the user-approved reason.

transitionDeviation.description: Optional. Records when this slide intentionally departs from the deck's transitionStrategy default visual contrast (visual-tone-mapping.md § Dimension 4 Earned exceptions). Required when the slide's contrast pattern does not match the strategy's default — never block to ask the user, just log it.

from.description: The deck-level transitionStrategy this slide deviates from.

appliedInstead.description: Short label for the contrast move actually applied (e.g. 'asymmetric-callback', 'snap-to-contrast', 'motif-break').

narrativeJustification.description: Why the deviation reinforces the slide's narrativeRole or storytelling beat. Generic reasons like 'visual variety' are rejected — must reference the specific narrative moment that demands the break.

designRationale.description: Why this layout was chosen. Body slides MUST use the structured object form (narrative + narrativeLink + focalPoint + eyePath + aestheticMove). The legacy 'aesthetic' string field is accepted for backward compatibility but emits a deprecation warning when used without aestheticMove.

layoutSpec.description: Required. Precise visual specification for this slide: background and content/decorative zones with coordinates. The LLM designs this from content structure, narrative role, and aesthetic signals, guided by layout-principles.md. Decorative elements (accent bars, side stripes, card backgrounds, etc.) are encoded as inline shape zones — there is no separate decoration catalog to reference.

contentBrief.description: Short description of what this slide should contain, guiding content fitting in Step 6.

LayoutSpec.description: LLM-designed visual layout specification for a single slide. Every visual element — content text, decorative shapes, images, charts — is expressed as a typed zone with explicit coordinates.

oneOf.description: Palette key for solid background (backward compat, e.g. 'primary', 'background').
Typed background specification: solid, gradient, or tinted.

background.description: Slide background. String = solid fill (legacy). Object = typed spec with solid/gradient/tinted.

zones.description: Content and decorative zones with precise positioning. Decorative elements (accent bars, stripes, card backgrounds, dividers, corner blocks, etc.) are zones with type='shape' and a fill — there is no separate decorations field, no reference into a decoration catalog.

isHero.description: Required for any slide intended to function as a hero (dramatic background, display typography, large media zone, full-canvas chart, etc.). The Universal Design Floor Quota 2 (hero cadence) reads this flag directly — there is no pattern-name fallback. If the LLM intends a slide to be hero-level, it MUST set isHero=true.

Zone.description: A positioned content or decorative zone within a slide.

role.description: Content slot identifier. Maps to zone name in s06-slide-content.json slots.

type.description: Zone type determining which python-pptx API is used: text→add_textbox, shape→add_shape, chart→add_chart, image→add_picture/add_image_safe, icon→add_textbox with icon font, formula→add_formula (mathtext→PNG; see sessions/formula-svg-design-2026-05-01.md).

x.description: Horizontal position in inches from left edge. Bare numbers are inches; '<N>in' strings are inches (explicit); '<N>%' strings are percent of slide width.

y.description: Vertical position in inches from top edge. Bare numbers are inches; '<N>in' strings are inches (explicit); '<N>%' strings are percent of slide height.

w.description: Width in inches. Bare numbers are inches; '<N>in' / '<N>%' supported per the same convention as x.

h.description: Height in inches. Bare numbers are inches; '<N>in' / '<N>%' supported per the same convention as y.

font.description: Font family key resolved from style-policy typography.

color.description: Palette key or direct hex color for text.

fill.description: Palette key for shape fill color.

source.description: Resource source. For type='image': 'user-media'=existing file from mediaAttachments; 'generated'=LLM-authored image created in Step 5f. For type='formula' (Phase 1): 'authored'=expression body comes from the matching s06 slot's formulaSource. Required for type='image' and type='formula' zones.

imageRequest.description: For source='generated' image zones: generation request consumed by image-generator skill. See workflow-step5.md § 6f Prompt authoring rules.

fallback.description: Required for source='generated' image zones. Declarative degradation path when generation fails. Either text-reflow (expand adjacent text zone) or shape replacement (decorative fill).

path.description: Resolved image path (relative to session). For user-media: set at design time. For generated: null pre-Step-6f, populated by s05f_patch_image_paths.py after VLM selection.

fallbackApplied.description: True when generation failed and s07-build.py should render the declared fallback instead of attempting to load `path`.

vlmSelectionApplied.description: True when the resolved `path` came from a VLM-curated s05f-image-selection.json group; False when s05f_patch_image_paths.py auto-picked the lowest-numbered variation. Step 8 QA should visually verify zones marked false.

alt.description: Alt-text for image zones, used as the image's alt-text attribute in the PPTX.

imageRole.description: Decorative weight class for image zones (Phase B accent-imagery model). 'atmospheric' = full-bleed background image (>= 90% canvas, requires scrim per Imagery Floor); 'narrative' = main subject image carrying meaning (35-70% canvas); 'accent' = small decorative motif/texture/wash (5-30% canvas, must clear title zone, prompt must be abstract/non-representational). When omitted on a generated image zone, the validator infers from area: >= 90% -> atmospheric, 35-90% -> narrative, < 35% -> accent.

runs.description: For text zones implementing the 'mixed-weight-run' title-choreography move. When present, the renderer should call s07_slide_helpers.add_textbox_runs() instead of add_textbox(). Each run renders as a separate <a:r> in the same paragraph; use {"text": "\n"} as a run to start a new paragraph. Mutually exclusive with the slot's plain `text` for that zone.

font.description: Palette typography key ('heading'/'body'/'mono') or explicit font name.

rotation.description: For text zones implementing 'vertical-act-label'. Common values: 270 (text reads bottom-to-top, left edge); 90 (top-to-bottom, right edge). Mapped at render time to the bodyPr `vert` attribute, not a shape rotation, so the zone's x/y/w/h describe the rotated visual footprint.

clipShape.description: Shape to clip the image to. 'circle'=oval clip via MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE.OVAL picture fill; 'hexagon'=hexagonal clip. 'none' or omitted = rectangular (no clipping).

notation.description: For type='formula' zones: math notation system. Phase 1: only 'mathtext' (matplotlib's LaTeX subset — fractions, roots, sub/sup, Greek, sums/integrals; no \begin{matrix}). Reserved for 'latex' / 'latex-omml' in later phases.

fitMode.description: For type='formula' zones: how the rendered formula sizes inside the zone. 'scale-to-zone' (default) preserves aspect and fills the zone. 'natural' keeps the rendered point size; helper auto-promotes to scale-to-zone when natural extent < 40% of zone (logged in build report).

verticalAlign.description: For type='formula' zones: vertical alignment of the rendered artifact within the zone (default 'middle'). For text zones use 'valign' instead.

alignment.description: For type='formula' zones: horizontal alignment of the rendered artifact within the zone (default 'center'). For text zones use 'align' instead.

colorToken.description: For type='formula' zones: palette token to bake as the formula color (e.g. 'ink', 'accent'). When omitted, the helper inherits the body-text color via the chain palette.body → palette.ink → #222222 so the formula reads as part of the surrounding prose.

fontPt.description: For type='formula' zones: rendering font size in points (default 24). Affects only the rasterized glyph density; final on-slide size is governed by fitMode.

BackgroundSpec.description: Typed background specification supporting solid, gradient, tinted, and image-generated backgrounds.

type.description: Background type. solid=flat fill, gradient=two-color directional blend, tinted=subtle color wash over a base, image-generated=full-bleed LLM-authored image (requires imageRequest + fallback per workflow-step5.md § 6f).

colors.description: Two palette keys for gradient backgrounds (e.g. ['primary', 'accent']).

base.description: Base palette key for tinted backgrounds (e.g. 'background').

tint.description: Tint palette key overlaid on base (e.g. 'accent').

tintOpacity.description: Tint opacity (0.01-0.20). Subtle wash, not a color fill.

imageRequest.description: Required for type='image-generated'. Authored in Step 5f and consumed by the image-generator skill. See workflow-step5.md § 6f Prompt authoring rules.

fallback.description: Required for type='image-generated'. Declarative degradation path so s07-build.py can render a coherent slide when generation fails. Typically a gradient or solid background spec.

path.description: Resolved image path (relative to session). Null pre-Step-6f; populated by s05f_patch_image_paths.py after VLM selection.

fallbackApplied.description: True when generation failed and s07-build.py should render the declared fallback instead of attempting to load `path`.

vlmSelectionApplied.description: True when the background's resolved `path` came from a VLM-curated selection; False when auto-picked from raw generation output.

fit.description: Optional, applies to type='image-generated'. 'cover' (default, recommended for full-bleed) fills the canvas and may crop overflow. 'contain' fits the entire image and may letterbox — only when preserving the whole image is required.

scrim.description: Optional full-bleed legibility overlay painted on top of an image-generated background. Decide per slide based on overlaid text color, image luminance, and visual register — do not hardcode for the deck. Omit for no scrim.

color.description: Palette key or hex string. Black-family for darkening under light text; white-family for lightening under dark text.

alpha.description: Opacity 0.0–0.85. Typical legibility values: 0.25–0.45 editorial; 0.55–0.75 hero with bold text over busy imagery.

rationale.description: Short note on why this scrim choice — references text color, image luminance, or register.
